book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice.
The Canons of the CCXVII Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage. Commonly Called The Code of Canons of the African Church.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

title: Canon XXXIII.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Canon XXXIII.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: (Greek xxxvi.)

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: That presbyters should not sell the goods of the Church in which they are constituted; and that no bishop can rightly use anything the title to which vests in the ecclesiastical maternal centre (μάτρικος).

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: It also seemed good that presbyters should not sell the ecclesiastical property where they are settled without their bishop’s knowledge; and it is not lawful for bishops to sell the goods of the Church without the council or their presbyters being aware of it.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Nor should the bishop without necessity usurp the property of the maternal (matricis) Church [nor should a presbyter usurp the property of his own cure (tituli)].

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Ancient Epitome of Canon XXXIII.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: A presbyter is not to sell ecclesiastical property without the consent of the bishop.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: A bishop is not to sell without the approbation of his synod a country property.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Fuchs (Biblioth. der Kirchenvers., vol. iij., p.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: 5) thinks the text is corrupt in the last sentence and should be corrected by Canon x. of the Council of Carthage of 421, so as to read, “that which is left by will to a rural church in the diocese must not be applied to the Mother Church through the usurpation of the bishop.”

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: So I turn the Lat.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Titulus for want of a proper English word.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: It denotes a lesser church in any city or diocese, served by a priest.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: “The Mother Church,” i.e., The cathedral, the Church in which the bishop resides.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Moreover at this Synod we read all the conciliar decrees of all the Province of Africa in the different synods held in the time of Bishop Aurelius.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Concerning the Synod which assembled in Hippo Regio.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Under the most illustrious consuls, the most glorious Emperor Theodosius Augustus for the third time, and Abundantius, on the viij.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Ides of October, at Hippo Regio, in the secretarium of the Church of Peace.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: And the rest of the acts of this Synod have not been written down here because these constitutions are found set forth above.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Of the Council of Carthage at which the proconsular bishops were appointed legates to the Council at Adrumetum.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: In the consulate of the most glorious emperors—Arcadius for the third time and Honorius for the second time, Augustuses, on the vith day before the Calends of July, at Carthage.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: In this council the proconsular bishops were chosen as legates to the Council of Adrumetum.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Of a Council of Carthage at which many statutes were made.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: In the consulate of those most illustrious men, Cæsarius and Atticus, on the vth day before the Calends of September in the secretarium of the restored basilica, when Aurelius the bishop, together with the bishops, had taken his seat, the deacons also standing by, and Victor the old man of Puppiana, Tutus of Migirpa and Evangel of Assuri.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: The Allocution of Aurelius the bishop of Carthage to the bishops.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Aurelius, the bishop, said: After the day fixed for the council, as ye remember, most blessed brethren, we sat and waited for the legations of all the African provinces to assemble upon the day, as I have said, set by our missive; but when the letter of our Byzacene bishops had been read, that was read to your charity, which they had discussed with me who had anticipated the time and day of the council; also it was read by our brethren Honoratus and Urban, who are to-day present with us in this council, sent as the legation of the Sitifensine Province.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: For our brother Reginus of the Vege [t]selitane Church, the letters sent to my littleness by Crescentian and Aurelius, our fellow-bishops, of the first sees of the [two] Numidias, in which writings your charity will see with me how they promised that either they themselves would be good enough to come or else that they would send legates according to custom to this council; but this it seems they did not do at all, the legates of Mauritania Sitifensis, who had come so great a distance gave notice that they could stay no longer; and, therefore, brethren, if it seem good to your charity, let the letters of our Byzacene brethren, as also the breviary, which they joined to the same letter, be read to this assembly, so that if by any chance they are not entirely satisfactory to your charity, such things in the breviary may be changed for the better after diligent examination.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: For this very thing our brother and fellow-bishop of the primatial see, a man justly conspicuous for his gravity and prudence, Mizonius, demanded in a letter he addressed to my littleness.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: If therefore it meets with your approval, let there be read the things which have been adopted and let each by itself be considered by your charity.
